read: The earlier 5.97-6.90 ms TileLang numbers are dKV-only/materialized-input sibling surfaces, not an internal slice of the 16.9 ms fused source-boundary dQ+dKV kernel. The same-kernel split shows the route-owned TileLang final dKV atomic write is not the wall after GP256 local grouped reduction; it is only about 0.047 ms. The fused TileLang wall is instead before final writeback: the route-owned source-boundary producer spends about 14.33 ms on dQ/recompute and another 2.50 ms on dKV contribution math plus in-CTA grouped reduction. This differs from the Triton query-major split, where the global dKV accumulation-like portion was about 8.2 ms. Therefore the current TileLang route-owned fused kernel is not a faster replacement for the Triton sparse-attention backward kernel; the next design needs a query-major owner schedule with a group-aware dKV epilogue, not more final-atomic tuning on this route-owned schedule.
decision: Do not claim the TileLang 5.97-6.90 ms dKV-only result materializes inside the fused sparse-attention backward kernel. Retain the same-kernel breakdown as the gate. The next kernel branch should keep Triton-like/query-major reuse for score/prob/dQ recompute and add a group-aware dKV epilogue before global memory, rather than using the current route-owned TileLang source-boundary schedule.
